title: Impossible Smash Burger
intro: The American smash burger is about crust and timing. Inspired by Midwest flat-top cooking and the Oklahoma onion burger, this version builds flavor in layers: a well-seasoned patty, onions cooked until sweet with a little bite left, and real white American cheese melted just enough to drape. Flipping the patties onto the onions at the end brings sweetness and moisture without softening the crust.

ingredientGroups.title: Burger Sauce
items: ½ cup reduced-fat mayonnaise
1 tablespoon ketchup
1 tablespoon finely minced dill pickle
1 teaspoon yellow mustard
½ teaspoon white vinegar
¼ teaspoon garlic powder
¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper

ingredientGroups.title: Patty Mix
items: 16 ounces Impossible ground "beef"
1 large egg white
½ cup panko breadcrumbs
1 tablespoon olive oil
1 teaspoon garlic powder
1 teaspoon onion powder
1 teaspoon cayenne pepper
1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
½ teaspoon table salt
½ teaspoon Sazón Total seasoning (Iberia brand used here)

ingredientGroups.title: Onions
items: 1 medium yellow onion, halved and thinly slivered
1 teaspoon avocado oil

ingredientGroups.title: Cooking
items: ½ teaspoon avocado oil

ingredientGroups.title: Assembly
items: 2 potato buns
Dill pickle slices
4 thin slices reduced fat white American cheese

stepGroups.title: Make the Burger Sauce
items: In a small bowl, combine reduced-fat mayonnaise, ketchup, minced pickle, mustard, vinegar, garlic powder, and black pepper.
Stir until smooth and evenly blended.
Refrigerate at least 15 minutes before using.
This recipe makes more sauce than needed for two sandwiches; store the remainder refrigerated for up to 5 days.

stepGroups.title: Build and Chill the Patty Mix
items: In a chilled mixing bowl, combine all patty mix ingredients.
Fold gently until evenly distributed. Do not knead or compress.
Stop mixing as soon as the texture looks uniform.
Press the mixture into a flat slab about 1 to 1½ inches thick in the bowl.
Cover and place in the freezer for 12 to 15 minutes.
The mixture should feel firm but not frozen.
Remove and proceed immediately to portioning.

stepGroups.title: Portion and Form
items: Divide the chilled mixture into 1.5 ounce portions. You should have 10 portions.
Using a 3¾-inch ring mold, press each portion into an even patty about 3/8 inch thick.
Apply just enough pressure to reach the edges of the ring without compacting tightly.
Place formed patties on a parchment-lined tray.
Refrigerate 20 to 30 minutes before cooking.
To freeze extras, place the tray in the freezer until firm, then transfer to a freezer bag with parchment between layers.
Patties may be cooked directly from frozen.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Onions (Pan One)
items: Place one cast iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add 1 teaspoon avocado oil and swirl to coat lightly.
Add the slivered onions and spread into a thin, even layer.
Cook 4 to 6 minutes, leaving undisturbed for 60 to 90 seconds at a time before flipping sections rather than stirring constantly.
Stop when about 60% of the onions are soft and translucent, 30% are lightly golden at the edges, and 10% remain slightly firm.
Divide into four thin piles and reduce heat to low to keep warm.

stepGroups.title: Smash and Finish (Pan Two)
items: Place a second cast iron skillet over high heat and preheat at least 5 minutes until very hot and just beginning to smoke.
Add ½ teaspoon avocado oil and wipe into a very thin film.
Place cold patties directly onto the hot surface.
Wait 8 to 10 seconds before pressing.
Press firmly for 5 seconds to increase surface contact. Do not twist. Do not thin beyond the formed shape.
Cook 55 to 65 seconds on the first side until deeply browned with slightly lacy edges.
Scrape confidently and flip.
Cook 20 to 30 seconds on the second side.
Transfer each patty directly onto an onion pile in the first pan.
Press gently 2 to 3 seconds to seat.
Top each patty with a slice of white American cheese and cover the onion pan for 20 to 30 seconds to melt.
Remove from heat promptly.

stepGroups.title: Assembly
items: Lightly toast the buns cut-side down until golden.
Place pickle slices on the bottom bun in a single layer.
Add the first patty with onions.
Add the second patty with onions, aligning for full bun coverage.
Spread burger sauce on the underside of the top bun.
Close the sandwich and press gently to seat the layers.
Serve immediately.
